title: Location-Area Validation, Encounter Relations, Filters, and Provenance · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript Pokedex interaction contains one plausible readline, token, type, validation, URL, fetch, promise, cache, abort, persistence, security, test, package, or release defect; find the first runtime or compile-time boundary failure before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in validated provenance-rich encounter explorer and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Distinguish region, location, and location-area endpoints and choose the relation that answers the stakeholder question.
Validate area identity and every nested Pokemon, version detail, method, condition, chance, and level shape needed by the view.
Deduplicate only chosen display rows while retaining source area, version, method, chance, conditions, and original identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 2741 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit nested location-area encounter relations and preserve filtered provenance under budgets; each Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, and handle needs explicit ownership; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish region, location, and location-area endpoints and choose the relation that answers the stakeholder question.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-ts-explore-changed-fixtures-fnd1hs" that uses case 3891 to admit nested location-area encounter relations and preserve filtered provenance under budgets. Keep its defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real signals, leave timers or Promise rejections, or claim native runtime, load, accessibility, packaging, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with TypeScript 7.0.2, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility surface where required, Node 24.18.0, original unknown fixtures, controlled fetch and stream adapters, emitted-runtime and subprocess tests, temporary files, package inspection, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using test and empty plus specific case evidence.
